artPrompt: An evocative scene of superstitious magic: a knuckled goblin fist rapping hard on a scorched wooden tabletop sends a shockwave of warding flame-orange light that bats away an incoming bolt, the fist foreshortened enormous toward the viewer, grain of the wood glowing along the impact. A bonfire flickers behind in night-black smoke, sparks and dice scattering, lucky desperate energy. Rendered as a bold painted graphic-novel splash — strong silhouettes, heavy directional inking and dramatic shadow shapes, fully painted (not flat cel comic).
name: Knock on Wood
text:
Whenever a spell or ability would deal damage to you, you may knock on the table (the actual table, with your actual knuckles, audibly) to prevent that damage. You may only do this if you genuinely believe in luck. The table knows if you're faking.
At the beginning of your upkeep, knock once for good fortune. If you forget, you have insulted the table, and it deals 1 damage to you. The table is petty.
flavorText: Whateverville was built on hardwood and harder luck.
